my_thoughts: The validation/definition works: physiological effect (laughter = physiological effect), defines: release mechanism (relaxation of tension from contradictory impression). The student reports observation (seeking explanation for somatic effect), which shows they understand: laughter = physiological effect (observation). They'll likely accept the validation/definition. Single-turn episode: observation report → validation/definition → complete. Hypothesis: somatic effect validations lead to compliance in single-turn episodes—testable by tracking whether somatic effect validations end episodes.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student reports observation ('Laughter seems to have a physiological effect')—seeking explanation for somatic effect via reports observation and links phenomenon to body. They understand: laughter = physiological effect (observation). The causal_chain mechanism validates: physiological effect (laughter = physiological effect), defines: release mechanism (relaxation of tension from contradictory impression). Pattern: observation report → validation/definition → [complete]. Low confidence because validations can lead to compliance or deeper questions.
visible_response:
Yes, maybe a contradictory impression that cannot be harmonized
produces a tension, and laughter relaxes it.
